description: PubMed references for the POMC->ACTH->alpha-MSH prohormone-cleavage cascade (NB2 Step 4 cleaved_from edges). POMC is proteolytically processed by prohormone convertases PC1/3 (to ACTH) and PC2 (ACTH to alpha-MSH). Source: PubMed. Retrieved via the PubMed connector for the reproducibility re-freeze of data/external/db_responses/ (original file absent from disk and git; see internal/CHANGELOG.md 2026-07-11T23:57Z).

8380577.title: The prohormone convertases PC1 and PC2 mediate distinct endoproteolytic cleavages in a strict temporal order during proopiomelanocortin biosynthetic processing.

8070378.title: In vitro processing of proopiomelanocortin by recombinant PC1 (SPC3).
8070378.step: POMC->ACTH (recombinant PC1 cleaves POMC to ACTH-related peptides)

8389457.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A
8389457.title: Ontogeny of the prohormone convertases PC1 and PC2 in the mouse hypophysis and their colocalization with corticotropin and alpha-melanotropin.
8389457.step: ACTH->alpha-MSH (PC2 in melanotropes; alpha-melanotropin as end product)

8822269.title: Processing of pro-opiomelanocortin in GH3 cells: inhibition by prohormone convertase 2 (PC2) antisense mRNA.
8822269.step: ACTH->alpha-MSH (PC2 is the convertase generating alpha-MSH)
